description:
Symptom: after bl-a2ea (projection-shape + accessor key fix) landed and was installed, a fresh sync correctly created 28 single-wrapped canonical balls tasks. Of those, 12 were release-chore issues already state=closed on GH (v0.3.1 through v0.4.0 — all PRs merged, all tags shipped) and 15 were trackers whose underlying work has shipped on main as [bl-c972]/[bl-2fe1]/[bl-650b]/[bl-313a] or in balls-github-plugin as [bl-a2ea] but whose GH issues were never closed.

bl-a2ea's writeup promised: 'once the fix lands and a sync runs, KnownUpdate emissions should drive those to status=closed automatically without manual intervention'. That did not happen across two post-install syncs. All 27 stayed at status=open and had to be archived by hand (see closing notes on bl-6d9e bl-98bc bl-c2ef bl-5068 bl-9c5f bl-677a bl-bca0 bl-7b04 bl-4615 bl-4a90 bl-e6f8 bl-bbea for the release-chore set and bl-495d bl-0552 bl-ccfb bl-5ebe bl-3608 bl-052e bl-73a4 bl-26e3 bl-8c43 bl-e85a bl-782f bl-98cc bl-7f56 bl-085f bl-7614 for the tracker set).

Hypothesis: pull::classify only emits KnownUpdate when the GH issue's state DIFFERS from the last_synced_status recorded on the task. For a SyncCreate that was born from a state=closed issue, last_synced_status is set to 'closed' at creation. On the next sync, GH state is still 'closed' and last_synced_status is still 'closed' — no transition, no KnownUpdate, no close-mirror drives the balls task from open to closed.

The original projection bug (bl-a2ea) just masked this: every poll auto-created a fresh duplicate, so close-mirror's failure mode was indistinguishable from the broader breakage. Now that classify works, this hole is the only thing preventing 'create from a closed GH issue' from yielding a closed balls task.

Fix shape: at SyncCreate time, if the incoming projection's issue.state == 'closed', either (a) set the new task's status=closed immediately during apply_created in balls-core, or (b) have pull_emit emit both a SyncCreate AND an immediate KnownUpdate{status=closed} for the same task in the same SyncReport. (a) is simpler and avoids the special case where last_synced_status==state. Whichever path: add a regression test where pull observes a state=closed issue not yet in balls and verifies the resulting balls task is created at status=closed.
title: github-issues: close-mirror doesn't fire for born-closed GH issues
